pr.title: ft/ftKirby: decompile 7 Kirby special N functions
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Decompiled seven Kirby neutral-special-related functions across copied ability handlers, swallow/drink animation handlers, aerial Jigglypuff Rollout startup, Mewtwo Shadow Ball copied-action setup, Sheik Needle Storm loop IASA, and Kirby hat material-animation loading. The PR replaced placeholder `/// #...` stubs with matching C implementations and adjusted the `ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C` header prototype from `UNK_RET` to `void`. The PR body states all seven functions were 100% matching by overnight script.
what_changed: Implemented `ftKb_SpecialNDrink0_Anim`, `ftKb_SpecialNDrink1_Anim`, and `ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim` in `ftKb_SpecialN.c`, handling swallow/drink command variables, victim or target item cleanup, sound playback, state transitions, and end-of-animation fall or wait transitions.

Implemented `ftKb_SkSpecialNLoop_IASA` and `ftKb_SkSpecialAirNLoop_IASA` in `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c`, checking B-button release to enter Sheik copied neutral-B end states and LR input to enter cancel states.

Implemented `ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568` in `ftKb_SpecialNZd.c` through a local static inline helper `ftKb_SpecialNMt_ChangeAction`, initializing copied Mewtwo neutral-B start motion, command variables, velocities, callbacks, charge/timer fields, and animation setup.

Implemented `ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim` in `ftKb_SpecialNNs.c`, transitioning copied Jigglypuff aerial Rollout startup into `ftKb_MS_PrSpecialAirNFull` after animation completion and setting callbacks, anim rate, velocity, and part rotation.
Implemented `ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C` in `ftKb_Init.c`, iterating Kirby hat material animation joints and adding material animations to hat DObjs based on costume data and part availability.
Added needed includes such as `ft/ftanim.h`, `ftCo_Throw.h`, and `ftCo_ThrownKirby.h` to support the newly decompiled logic.
smart_moves: Used a small `static inline` helper, `ftKb_SpecialNMt_ChangeAction`, for the copied Mewtwo action setup, likely to preserve matching structure while keeping the exported function body minimal.

Preserved known matching idioms such as `(0, GET_FIGHTER(gobj))`, explicit `PAD_STACK(8)` and `PAD_STACK(32)`, nested local scopes, and local re-fetches of `Fighter*` where required by the target code shape.
Changed the header prototype to the discovered concrete return type only after implementing `ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C`, reducing `UNK_RET` usage.
Kept ground and air Sheik IASA implementations parallel but with the correct distinct motion states: `ftKb_MS_SkSpecialNEnd`/`Cancel` versus `ftKb_MS_SkSpecialAirNEnd`/`Cancel`.

For Kirby hat material animation loading, modeled the anonymous costume-data layout locally with `HSD_Joint*` and `HSD_MatAnimJoint*`, avoiding a broader struct commitment where the slice only proves this local shape.
decomp_lessons: Kirby copied neutral-special code often uses the `mv.kb.specialhi` field group even for non-up-special copied ability state, so field names may reflect shared storage rather than semantic move names.

Animation handlers commonly guard one-time event logic with `fp->cmd_vars[0]`, clear related object pointers after consuming the event, then separately check `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining` for the state transition.

Ground and air versions of copied ability handlers can be near-identical but must preserve float literal spelling and integer literal spelling where matching requires it, as seen in `0.0F, 1.0F, 0.0F` versus `0, 1, 0` call arguments.

When decompiling Kirby copy/hat code, costume-specific data and material animation traversal may require local temporary struct definitions and calls to `ftAnim_GetNextMatAnimJointInTree` rather than fully named global types.

Callback restoration is a recurring part of copied Kirby state transitions; decompiled functions set callbacks such as `death2_cb`, `take_dmg_cb`, `deal_dmg_cb`, `x21F8`, and `accessory4_cb` alongside motion-state changes.
Matching may require stack padding even in apparently straightforward logic; this PR used `PAD_STACK(8)` and `PAD_STACK(32)` in multiple functions.
naming_conventions: Kirby copied-special functions keep character-copy abbreviations in the function name, such as `Sk` for Sheik, `Mt` for Mewtwo, and `Pr` for Purin/Jigglypuff.
Motion-state names mirror the copied character and ground/air variants, e.g. `ftKb_MS_SkSpecialNEnd`, `ftKb_MS_SkSpecialAirNEnd`, `ftKb_MS_MtSpecialNStart`, and `ftKb_MS_PrSpecialAirNFull`.

IASA handlers use the suffix `_IASA`; animation callbacks use `_Anim`; internal address-suffixed functions remain named with their address when semantics are not yet fully named, such as `ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C` and `ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568`.
The PR retained established mixed fighter access macro/function usage, including both `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` and `getFighter(gobj)`, rather than normalizing names at the cost of matching.
Header cleanup replaced `UNK_RET` with `void` where the implementation established no returned value.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used comma-expression fighter access `(0, GET_FIGHTER(gobj))` in `ftKb_SpecialNDrink0_Anim` and `ftKb_SpecialNDrink1_Anim`.
Inserted `PAD_STACK` macros to match stack layout in `ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C`, `ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim`, `ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim`, and `ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568`.
Introduced a `static inline` helper for `ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568`, suggesting the target assembly matched better with extracted inline action-change setup.
Used a block-scoped anonymous struct pointer cast for `ftKb_Init_803C9FC8[arg1]` costume data in the hat material-animation loader.

Preserved redundant-looking local variables and re-fetches, such as `fp2 = getFighter(gobj)` after victim interaction in `ftKb_SpecialAirNDrink_Anim`, which may be necessary for register allocation/order.
Used a `static u32 const mf = (1 << 1) | (1 << 4) | (1 << 18)` motion flag expression in `ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim` instead of replacing it with a less evidenced symbolic flag combination.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body reports verification: all seven functions were 100% matching by overnight script.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type `ftKb_SpecialN_800EF35C` and the local costume-data structure used for Kirby hat material animations.
Search for additional Kirby copied Mewtwo functions around `ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107410` and `ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107638` to see whether `mv.kb.specialhi` fields receive better names.
Compare other copied Sheik Kirby IASA or accessory callbacks involving `fn_80106DB0` for naming and callback semantics.
Look for future cleanup of `ftKb_PrSpecialAirNStart_Anim` motion flags and callbacks to replace raw bit expressions or unknown callback names.
Search for other PRs using `(0, GET_FIGHTER(gobj))` and `PAD_STACK` in Kirby animation handlers as matching reference patterns.
